seoTitle: Glyde Point vs Gray — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Glyde Point and Gray. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Glyde Point vs Gray: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Glyde Point and Gray.
heading: Glyde Point vs Gray — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Glyde Point, Northern Territory and Gray, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gray has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Glyde Point (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Glyde Point or Gray safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Glyde Point has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Gray scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Glyde Point scores 60/100 for bushfire risk. Gray scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Glyde Point or Gray?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gray has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
